nodes.context: Palestinian militant organization that received funds and weapons from the Blekingegade Gang.
Danish Maoist political party; the gang referred to themselves as its inner core.

Successor organization to KAK; the gang referred to themselves as its inner core.

German militant group; mentioned in context of joint hijacking with PFLP.

edges.target: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
edges.evidence_quote: Between December 1972 and May 1989, they committed robberies and sent the money to the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine.
edges.context: The gang funded the PFLP through robberies.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang referred to themselves as the inner core of three organizations named Kommunistisk Arbejdskreds
edges.context: The gang considered themselves the inner core of KAK.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang referred to themselves as the inner core of three organizations named ... Kommunistisk Ungdoms Forbund
edges.context: The gang considered themselves the inner core of KUF.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang referred to themselves as the inner core of three organizations named ... Manifest-Kommunistisk Arbejdsgruppe
edges.context: The gang considered themselves the inner core of M-KA.

edges.evidence_quote: The meeting was to evaluate the political relevance of cooperating with Wadi Haddad's PFLP-EO in addition to George Habash's PFLP.
